title: HttpSequence
description: A comprehensive schema for defining a sequence of HTTP requests. It's designed for API testing and automation, allowing for complex workflows with multiple steps, various authentication methods, and post-request processing.

name.description: The name of the sequence. Used for identification and logging.

baseUrl.description: An optional base URL to be prepended to all request endpoints in the sequence. If a step's endpoint is a full URL, this base URL is ignored for that step.

steps.description: The ordered list of steps to be executed in the sequence.

name.description: The unique name of this step. Used as a key to store conversation results.

request.description: Defines the HTTP request to be executed in this step.

method.description: The HTTP method for the request.

endpoint.description: The request's endpoint. It can be a relative path that will be appended to the `baseUrl` or a full URL, which will override the `baseUrl`.

bearerToken.description: Optional Bearer Token for authentication. This will add an 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header.

headers.description: A map of HTTP headers to be sent with the request.

queryParams.description: A map of query parameters to be appended to the URL.

payload.description: The request body, typically for methods like POST, PUT, or PATCH. Can be any valid JSON object, array, or primitive. For non-JSON payloads, consider encoding it as a string.

timeout.description: The timeout for the request in milliseconds. Defaults to 5000ms.

proxy.description: Optional proxy configuration for the request.

plugins.description: An optional list of plugins to execute after the request is completed. Useful for extracting data from the response or performing custom validation.

name.description: A descriptive name for the plugin instance.

type.description: The type of the plugin to be executed. This should correspond to a registered plugin.

args.description: A list of arguments to be passed to the plugin.
